enonce.texte: Read each sentence carefully and choose the correct social media vocabulary word to fill in the blank. Pay attention to the context (platform, action, or communication term).

questions.texte: To put a photo or a video on your Instagram profile, you usually click on the '______' button.
When you see an interesting article on Facebook, you can '______' it on your own timeline so your friends can see it.
If you want to save a nice picture on Pinterest for later, you '______' it to one of your boards.
On WhatsApp, when you write a quick message to a friend, you are sending a ______ message.
A ______ on social media is a direct or public reaction that can be positive (like a heart) or negative (like an angry face).
On Twitter (X), a single ______ cannot exceed 280 characters.

enonce.texte:
Read the paragraph below about a student in Cameroon. Then, answer the questions by applying your knowledge of social media vocabulary and communication.

Situation: Martin is a 'Première' student in Douala. He is addicted to social media. One day, his father takes his phone away because Martin spent 6 hours on TikTok instead of studying for his Probatoire exam. Martin feels lost. He wants to contact his study group on WhatsApp, check a video link his teacher sent on Facebook, and see the new photos his cousin posted on Instagram.

questions.texte: Identify the social media platform mentioned in the text that Martin uses for his study group.
What action did Martin's cousin do on Instagram with the new photos?

Imagine Martin gets his phone back for one hour. He wants to inform all his friends that he is organizing a revision session. He writes: "Revision session at my place on Saturday at 10 AM. Come with your notebooks."

**Evaluate** this message. Which important social media communication element is missing that could help him reach more people?
questions.reponse: A hashtag (#) or a mention (@) to alert specific friends or groups.

questions.texte:
Martin's father argues that social media is a distraction. Martin argues it is a learning tool.

**Defend** Martin's position by giving two examples of how the platforms mentioned (WhatsApp, Facebook, Instagram) could be used for educational purposes.
questions.reponse: 1. WhatsApp: sharing documents, creating study groups, or sending voice notes explaining lessons. 2. Facebook: following educational pages, watching tutorial videos, or joining subject-specific groups. 3. Instagram: following accounts that post daily grammar tips or historical facts.

questions.texte: **Construct** a short public post (two sentences maximum) that Martin could write on Facebook to ask his classmates for help with a difficult lesson on 'Present Perfect'. Use at least two of these words: comment, share, message, post, tag.
questions.reponse: (Example answer) 'Hello everyone, can someone please explain the Present Perfect tense to me? Please **comment** your explanations or **message** me directly if you have good notes.'
